findings.line: The crew needed hands, not apology.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 direct contrast pivot; the sentence survives when the negative half is removed.

findings.line: Sylvanas had come to stand beside the rail stanchion, not crowding, not touching. Only present.
findings.deletionTest: Sylvanas had come to stand beside the rail stanchion. Only present.
findings.reason: Pattern 7 chained negation plus fragment reveal; the negative half is scaffolding around the real beat.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas had come to stand beside the rail stanchion and wait there.

findings.line: Then she slid her magic into the same motion, not making a second engine so much as shaving the worst edge off the sea's refusal where it struck.
findings.deletionTest: Then she slid her magic into the same motion, shaving the worst edge off the sea's refusal where it struck.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 direct contrast pivot; the sentence improves when it starts with the actual action.
findings.suggestedFix: Then she slid her magic into the same motion, shaving the worst edge off the opposing drag where it struck.

findings.line: Too late for comfort, not too late to keep after Arthas.
findings.deletionTest: Still time to keep after Arthas.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 direct contrast pivot; the second half carries the meaning and the refused half is dramatic scaffolding.
findings.suggestedFix: Too late for comfort. Still time to keep after Arthas.

clusterWarnings: High-risk analytical POV section: magical-pressure passages repeatedly launder agency onto abstract force nouns.
Dedicated P5/P6 resweep confirmed repeated abstract-force-to-body and abstract-force-to-environment smears.

findings.line: One line, harder than before, rising out of the horizon and fastening somewhere behind her teeth.
findings.subject: line
findings.realAgent: Jaina's bodily perception of the northward pull tightening under the resonance.
findings.reason: Pass-A resweep hit: an abstract route signal is being settled into a body part instead of naming the bodily effect.
findings.suggestedFix: One line rose out of the horizon, harder than before. An ache set in behind her teeth.

findings.subject: water
findings.realAgent: the supernatural resistance in the route and wind
findings.reason: Pattern 5: the sentence gives refusal to the water instead of naming the hostile resistance acting through it.

findings.line: By midnight the sea had erased even the idea of coast.
findings.subject: sea
findings.realAgent: distance, darkness, and the loss of visible shore markers
findings.reason: Pattern 2: the scene gives the sea judgment-level agency instead of naming the visible conditions that removed the coast.
findings.suggestedFix: By midnight the coast was gone.

findings.line: Jaina released before the answering pressure could find her fully.
findings.subject: pressure
findings.realAgent: the retaliatory backlash running back along her spell line
findings.reason: Pattern 5: the pressure is given targeting behavior instead of the prose naming the backlash hitting her.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina released before the answering pressure hit full force.

findings.line: The wind found her at once.
findings.subject: wind
findings.realAgent: the hostile backlash striking her along the opened spell line
findings.reason: Pattern 5: the line gives the wind seeking behavior instead of naming the impact.
findings.suggestedFix: The wind hit her at once.

findings.line: The pressure she had been following no longer lived only in magic. It had entered air and water and light themselves, flattening all three into one harder world.
findings.subject: pressure
findings.realAgent: the source's ambient effect saturating the environment near Northrend
findings.reason: Pattern 5/9 hybrid: the abstract pressure behaves like an invading actor instead of the prose stating how the environment has changed.
findings.suggestedFix: The pressure she had been following no longer lived only in magic. Air, water, and light had all flattened into one harder world.

findings.line: The name did nothing to soften the place.
findings.subject: name
findings.realAgent: nothing in the visible cove changed when Dagny named it
findings.reason: Pattern 5: the sentence gives the name softening power instead of stating that the place stayed as harsh as it looked.

audits.cleanReason: Counted the full tic set. Tightened appeared once in a physical-use slot, eased appeared once in a bodily-recovery slot, eye/gaze beats stayed at three functional instances, and non-'said' performance tags stayed at one actual low-volume beat. No corner-of-mouth, marked, shifted-expression, or weight-metaphor cluster crossed a cap.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A still contains 11 KILLs, including 2 moderate intent-smear failures in the magical-pressure passages, so the chapter must return to slophunter revision.
